titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Gay Comics Collection
titlestmt.author: compiled by Staff of Manuscripts and Archives

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday November 13, 2020 at 10:23

did.abstract: The collection contains assorted lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender and queer (LGBTQ) comic publications dealing with issues such as coming out, romantic and sexual relationships, depression, and gay and lesbian liberation. Some magazines appear to be self published while others are commercially published.

persname.part: Ahern, Brian Douglas (Fortune favors the bold!)
Dean, Beth (Beth Dean is loose in San Francisco)
Dean, Beth (Beth Dean is pregnant with desire)
Denson, Abby (My boyfriend is a space robot!)
Hall, Justin, 1971- (Le Croisic : our night in a French phone booth)
Hall, Justin, 1971- (Swallowing a cobra's heart and other true travel tales)
Harrison, Pam, 1964- (House of the muses : the latter days of Sappho of Lesbos)
Mance, Ajuan (Little book of big black bears)
McWhinny, Sean Seamus (Diary of a catering whore)
Miller, Desmond (Hansel and Gretel : agents of the coven)
Wings, Mary, 1949- (Are your highs getting you down?)

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Transferred by the Larry Kramer Initiative Executive Coordinator, 2004-2005. Transferred from the Gay and Lesbian Studies librarian, 2015. Transferred from the Women's, Gender and Sexuality Studies and Gay and Lesbian Studies librarian, 2015.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Gay Comics Collection (MS 1851). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged alphabetically by title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection contains assorted lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender and queer (LGBTQ) comic publications dealing with issues such as coming out, romantic and sexual relationships, depression, and gay and lesbian liberation. Some magazines appear to be self published while others are commercially published.

title.part: Love minus zero and other stories

did.unittitle: The Adventures of Baby Dyke [Terry Sapp]
Are Your Highs Getting You Down? [Mary Wings]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: No. 1, signed by the artist, Tim Barela

title.part: Meatmen: An Anthology of Gay Male Comics

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This accession comprises assorted lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, and queer (LGBTQ) comics, including ones that are professionally published and self-published. The comics explore such topics as coming out, bullying, romantic and sexual relationships, experiences with transitioning and expressing gender identity, stereotypes of LGBTQ people, politics, and treatment by family and friends that are not LGBTQ identified. A significant portion of these comics are science fiction and fantasy stories. Some comics that are not explicitly related to LGBTQ topics are also included in this accession, possibly because of the LGBTQ identity of their creators. Publication dates range from 1985 to 2014, with the bulk from 2000 to 2014.

did.unittitle: The Auger [Josh Trujillo and Elliot Baggott]
Bakersfield, Earth [David Quantic and Tamra Bonvillain]
Beth Dean is Loose in San Francisco [Beth Dean]
Beth Dean is Pregnant with Desire [Beth Dean]
Diary of a Catering Whore [Sean Seamus McWhinny]
Fortune Favors the Bold! [Brian Douglas Ahern and Randall Kirby]
Friend of Dorothy [Brian Andersen and Neftali Centeno]
Hansel and Gretel: Agents of the Coven [Desmond Miller]
House of the Muses: The Latter Days of Sappho of Lesbos [Pam Harrison]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2-9, 15, all with variant covers, 2 and 3 signed by the artist

did.unittitle: Le Croisic: Our Night in a French Phone Booth [Justin Hall]
The Legend of Bold Riley [Leia Weathington]
The Legend of Hedgehog Boy [Rene Capone]
Liliane and Muesli in Beyond Labels and Shades of Queer [Leanne Franson]
Liliane and Muesli in le regarde des autres and Energy Saver [Leanne Franson]
Liliane and Muesli in Letting Work Slide! [Leanne Franson]
The Little Book of Big Black Bears [Ajuan Mance]

unittitle.emph: Love Minus Zero and Other Stories

did.unittitle: Lumberjanes [Shannon Watters, Grace Ellis, Noelle Stevenson, and Brooke Allen]
Mahou Shounen Fight! [Dusty K. Smith and JD Saxon]
My Boyfriend is a Space Robot! [Abby Denson]
Night Patrol [Kenne Morrison and Sean Freitas]
Pride High [Tommy Roddy and Brian Ponce]
Reignbow and Dee-Va [Brian Andersen and Celina Hernandez]
Sex and the Superhero [Brian Andersen, Neftali Centeno, Celina Hernandez]
Spectra: Turbulent Times [Rebecca Thompson and Kerry G. Johnson]
Swallowing a Cobra's Heart and Other True Travel Tales [Justin Hall]
Unabashedly Billie [Brian Andersen and Preston Nesbit]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This accession contains several volumes of Kurt Erichsen's comic Murphy's Manor . The comic follows five housemates, four gay men and a lesbian woman, who live in an unnamed city in Ohio. Topics include dating, politics, sex, and gay bar culture. Publication dates range from 1981 to 1993.

did.unittitle: Murphy's Manor: Meg for Mayor [Kurt Erichsen]
